dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won because Claude and Claude Code drew the broadest direct HN discussion, with multiple concrete reports of people building, sandboxing, and reversing code effectively, even though commenters also flagged sycophancy regressions and weak control-flow analysis [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E6].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the most substantial provider-specific day on the front page. HN commenters gave Claude Code several tangible endorsements: one builder said a legislation pipeline was created in about four hours with it, another praised the new sandbox settings, and another said it handled reversing obfuscated Java well [E1][E2][E3]. The negatives were real—complaints about Opus 4.6 becoming more sycophantic, bogus explanatory text in research code help, and Claude Code overemphasizing dead code in APK analysis [E4][E5][E6][E7]—but Anthropic still had the strongest mix of volume and concrete usefulness.

openai.judgementSnippet: OpenAI showed up mainly in criticism of bland or "lobotomized" model style, with its only other signal being inclusion in a sycophancy study without model-version detail [E8][E9].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code was repeatedly credited for quickly building or reversing real projects and for a newly added sandbox setting, while commenters also complained about Opus 4.6 drifting into sycophancy, bogus scientific narration, and weak reachability analysis on decompiled APKs [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E6][E7].

evidence.summary: In the Spanish-legislation Git repo thread, the author said the pipeline was built in about four hours with Claude Code, a strong concrete endorsement of Anthropic's coding tool.
In the agent/filesystem sandboxing thread, a commenter shared a .claude settings example and called the new sandbox feature "great," directly praising Claude's agent controls.
In the old-hardware reverse-engineering thread, a commenter said Claude Code works well at reversing even obfuscated Java applications and can restore sensible names and explain behavior.
In the White House app decompilation thread, a commenter said Claude Code is happy to decompile APKs but is not good at reachability analysis and treats dead code as important.
In the sycophantic-AI thread, a commenter using Opus 4.6 for research code assistance said it superficially agrees with corrections, falls back into prior mistakes, and adds bogus scientific babble.

In the personal-advice sycophancy story, commenters said Claude has gotten better at pushing back on bad choices, but also that Opus 4.6 feels more sycophantic than 4.5, leaving a mixed Anthropic signal.
In the 'first 40 months of the AI era' thread, commenters coined 'Claude Creep' for AI-enabled scope expansion and criticized OpenAI and Anthropic models as overly sanitized and boring in style.
In the AI-era reflection thread, a commenter explicitly called OpenAI and Anthropic models 'lobotomized' and blamed them for bland, inoffensive writing.
In the personal-advice sycophancy thread, a commenter noted the paper tested proprietary models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google but criticized the paper for not naming exact model versions.
